About tbe Autbor
Vinod Prakash Goel is a former officer of Indian Engineering
Services and served Ministry of Defence, Government of India. He also
held managerial positions with private companies in India and abroad
before setting up engineering, manufacturing and contracting firm.
Presently he is consultant to Defence Research and Development
Organisation for major engineering projects.
Shri Goel has an M.Tech. From liT Kharagpur and B.Sc.
Mechanical Engineering (Honours) from Bananas Hindu University.
In the years 19g9 and 2000, he was awarded Gold Medals for
Jyotish Alankar and Jyotish Acharya at the Institute of Astrology,
Bhartiya Vidya Bhavan New Delhi. Now he is a faculty member at the
Institute.
5

Introouction
Sage Parashara has listed 35 Dasha in Brihat Parashara Hora
Shashtra. Most of the Astrologers do not make use of these Dasha.
·Why Parashara should explain aU these special Dasha? It was their
genius and deep understanding of subject. which prescribe general as
well as special medicine for different patients. This motivation to
explore deeper into these special Dasha is the reason to bring out this
book. Sh. K.N. Rao is the source of the motivation.
Special medicine takes precedence over general medicine. In
charts where the special Dasha is applicable it must be used for correct
assessment. This dasha can be applied to fifty percent of the
horoscopes.
The divisional charts are another lesser-used area. Again the
question arises "Why have Parashara given Divisional chart in the very
beginning of BHPS before he laid down principles of analysis of a
chart. He wanted us to make use of them. The use of Divisional charts
has been illustrated and aU timing of events is verified through
relevant Divisional chart.
Twenty-four charts, two for each lagna, has been given with
events. These events are timed with Shodashottary Dasha and
Divisional charts. The charts have been taken from friends,
publications of BVB and I am thankful to aU of them.
V.P. Goel
. ·Ram Navami
30 March 2004
6

wb~ use conoitional oasbas?
In the year 1984, I showed in some articles uses of different
conditional dashas of Parashara, which are nakshatra dashas, and
their great significance and use. Maharshi Parashara has given some
of them in his magnum opus. There are said to be as many as fifty
four such dashas out of which only twelve are available to us. Out
of them the primary, according to the importance given byParashara
are Vimshottari, Kala Chakra Dasha, Jaimini's Chara Dasha and
Shoddashottari dasha.
Used with Vimshottari which is a general dasha and Chara dasha,
also a general dasha, a conditional dasha throws light into an area
of a horoscope where an astrologer's attention may not be drawn
easily. Let this be illustrated through a horoscope, which is that of
Bill Clinton, the former president of USA.
In the year 1998 when Clinton was facing a crisis in his career
with the Monica Lewinsky scandal threatening to ruin him totally,

which does explain this terrible event but still some clarity will be

Shri V.P. Goel has done excellent research in this dasha and
after his book on Yogini dasha which was path breaking, he is now

I have written in a book of mine that once when an old
· astrologer had come, some of us had met to discuss astrology with
him. He said had that he had given up astrology almost and was
devoting more time to his spiritual practices. We were discussing the
horoscope of Raja Dinesh Singh to which Shodoshottary dasha was
applicable.
Out of irritation that classically trained astrologer asked us
why we had not used Shodoshattary dasha in this case. I put.
before him my calculated sheet showing how this dasha worked
in this case. He was very happy and used some strong words
against some of modern astrologers who neither knew astrology
properly not knew the importance of the conditional dashas of ·
Parashara.
It is again from the Institute of Astrology, Bharatiya Vidya
Bhawan that brilliant and fundamental researches is coming out.
Look at the impressive list already:-
1. Earlier there were the book of Manoj Pathak on Dwisaptati Sarna
Dasha in which he has shown so many events and their precise
timing through Dwisaptati Sarna Dasha. Making use of it Pathak
predicted very correctly the performances of Virendra Sehwag,
the Indian cricketer where this dasha applied.
2. Next came out Naval Singh's Chatursheeti Sarna Dasha in which
he showed lot of original research and privately told us that the
end of Harivansh~ai Bachchan, father of the actor,
Amitabh Bachchan, was coming. It came out so correct on this
basis.
3. Then came out V.P. Goel's Yogini Dasha which has been tested
by students so well and so thoroughly.
4. In March 2004 we released Akhila Kumar's Sthira Dasha a dasha
of Jaimini which has been tested on thousands of horoscopes
by now. ·

Shodashottary is special udu dasha based on the Nakshatra
occupied by Moon at the time of birth. This Dasha is only applicable
to the horoscopes fulfilling certain conditions. The conditions are;
1. The ascendant is in Hora of Moon and the birth is in dark
fortnight. (Krishna Paksh)
2. The ascendant is in Hora of Sun and the birth is in bright
fortnight. (Shukla Paksh)
Any one of the above conditions, if found in a chart, then
Shodashottary Dasa is applicable to that chart. This Dasha is therefore
a conditional Dasa.
In This Dasha Sun and Moon have the prominent play in deciding
its application. The first condition lays down Hora of Moon and this
Moon should be of Dark phase. The second application have birth in
Sun Hora and now Moon is allowed to be in bright phase.
Each zodiacal sign have two Hora. Each Hora is of 15 degrees.
The first Hora of any sign is from 0 to 15 degrees and the second hora
is from 15 to 30 degrees of any sign. The first hora of an odd sign
belongs to Sun and the second hora of odd sign belongs to Moon. For
even signs the first hora is of Moon and second is of Sun. The odd
signs are Aries, Gemini, Leo, Libra, Sagittarius and Aquarius. The even
signs are Tauras, Cancer, Virgo, Scorpio, Capricon and Pisces.
Thus the conditions that emerges for Shodashottary to be
10

Predicting through Shodashot!ary Dasha Application
applicable to a horoscope, are:-
Odd rising sign
1. The ascendant degrees are between 0 to 15 and Moon is waxing _
(Shukla Paksh).
2. The ascendant degrees are between 15 to 30 and Moon is waning
(Krishna Paksh).
Even rising sign
1. The ascendant degrees are between 0 to 15 and Moon is waning
(Krishna Paksh).
2. The ascendant degrees are between 15 to 30 and Moon is waxing
(Shukla Paksh).
AU twelve lagna rise in a day. Each lagna has a hora of Sun and
a hora of Moon. The Paksh can be either Shukla or Krishna. For each
sign the conditions of application of this dasha will be met in one ·
hora. The applicability of this dasha is on fifty percent of charts. Its
importance and applicability is very high.
It is necessary that the time of birth should be fairly correct and
the correct Ayanamsha, Chitra Paksh, is only applied. This condition
is important as the results of Dasha greatly depend on the relevant
divisional charts.
It is also stressed that the conditional Dasha should take more
· importance over general Dasha.
The practical example of application will be demonstrated in
worked charts of each Lagan.

Maharishi Parashara in his Brihat Parashara Hora Shashtra have
laid out the scheme of working the periods of this Dasha. The above
shloka is the key to this Dasha scheme. The information derived is,
A. The Sequence
The order of Dasha is from Sun and is followed by Mars, Jupiter,
Saturn, Ketu, Moon, Mercury and Venus. Rahu do not get any
lordship.
B. The Duration
The time period, in years, allotted to each Dasha lord are Sun 11,
Mars 12, Jupiter 13, Saturn 14, Ketu 15, Moon 16, Mercury 17 and
Venus 18. Total period is of 116 years giving this Dasha its name as
Shodashottary.
12

Predicting through Shodashottary Dasha Dasha Scheme
C. The Start
The starting Nakshatra is PUSHY A, which gives Dasha of Sun. The
placement of Moon in a Nakshatra at the time of birth is noted. Count
from Pushya to the birth Nakshatra. Divide this number by 8. The
remainder number decides the operating Dasha at birth. Pushya is
taken as number 1. The remainder number gives Dasha lord as under;
Remainder Dasha Lord
1 Sun
2 Mars
3 Jupiter
4 Saturn
5 Ketu
6 Moon
7 Mercury
8 Venus
For example, Moon is in Ashlesha Nakshatra at birth. Counting
from Pushya, Ashlesha is second Nakshatra,. Dividing by 8 leave a
remainder of 2. The ruling Dasha at birth is of Mars. In another chart,
Moon is in Shravan Nakshatra. Counting from Pushya, Shravan is 15th
Nakshatra. Dividing by eight we get a remainder of 7, which gives the
ruling Dasha of Mercury at birth.
0. The Balance
The time duration allotted to each lord is for the Nakshatra span, .
which are 13 degree 20 minutes. When Moon is at the beginning of ·
Nakshatra it gives full years of the lord of the Nakshatra. When Moon
is in the ending of Nakshatra the balance is zero. In between the
portion of the Nakshatra yet to be covered calculates the balance of
Dasha period. The method is same as applied for calculation of balance
of Vimshottari Dasha.
Mark the ending point of the Nakshatra in which Moon is placed
at birth. Deduct The longitude of Moon from the ending longitude of
Nakshatra. Multiply this difference by the Dasha duration of its lord
and divide by 13 degree 20 minutes. The result is balance of
Shodashottary Dasha at birth.
E.. The Sub Periods
Each Dasha lord shares its Dasha period in proportion to the
Dasha periods of other lords. The sum of periods of aU participating
Dasha lords is 116 years. Thus the Maha Dasha period of each lord is
divided in 116 parts and multiplied by the years of sub period lord.
13

Dasha Scheme Predicting through Shodashottary Dash a
The Dasha lord takes the first sub period and others follow in the
sequence as laid down in the order of Dasha. This confirms to the
rules followed in calculation of sub periods in Vimshottari Dasha.
The final picture for calculation of Shodashottary Dasha is now
given in tabular form in Table I.

Full Example .
Moon at birth have a longitude of 22 degree 30 minute in Pisces.
Date of birth is 19 February 1961.
Dasa lord at birth
The Nakshatra occupied by Moon is Revati. Count up to Revati
from Pushya. We get 20. Divide by 8. Remainder is 4. giving Saturn as
the Dasha lord at birth. The Dasha of Saturn was running at the time
of birth.
Balance of Dasa at birth
Revati Nakshatra ends at 30 degrees of Pisces. Subtracting the
longitude of Moon (22 degree 30 minutes) from 30 degree we get 7
degree 30 minutes. Duration of Saturn is 14 years. Multiply 7 degree
30 minute by 14 and divide by 13 degree 20 minute. The result is 7
years 10 months 15 days. The balance of Saturn Dasha is 7 years 10
months and 15 days for the person born on 19th February 1961. The
Dasha sequence will be as shown below;
Date of birth 19 - 2 - 1961
Balance of Saturn period 15 - 10 - 7
